results.title: JiuJitsu.net — Rankings, Videos, News and More
results.content:
# About This App

JiuJitsu.net ranks competitors and provides a comprehensive database of matches in events run by the International Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu Federation. We are an independant site and are not affiliated with the IBJJF.

Competitors are rated using the Elo rating system, which is a method for calculating the relative skill levels of players in competitive games. We allow users to search for competitors, view their ratings, and see their match history.

This app is a work in progress and is being developed by Dan Lukehart and Will Weisser. The source code is available on GitHub. Ratings may be subject to change as the app is developed and improved. [...] We host and maintain this app as a free service to the jiu-jitsu community. If you would like to support us, please consider donating.

If you would like a copy of our source data for research purposes, contact us and we'll be happy to help. You have permission to use data or screenshots from this site in articles, blog posts, and other media, but we ask that you credit JiuJitsu.net. All other uses of our data, including commercial use, is prohibited without an explicit license.

# Full Disclaimer

While we only rank athletes who compete in the International Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu Federation, this site is not affiliated, endorsed by, or associated with the IBJJF. [...] Q: Are you going to track kids divisions?

A: We have decided to store a searchable list of teen matches as some of these athletes are beginning their serious competitive journey in the sport. However, we do not compute ratings for teens and we do not track younger divisions in order to promote a healthy competitive environment for children.

Q: What are the "Elite" badges on some athlete profiles?

A: We assign an "Elite" badge to athletes whose ratings are among the top competitors in the same belt, weight and age divisions (or across all weights in the same age, a.k.a. Pound-for-Pound / P4P). These badges are a mark of achievement and allow fans to easily recognize when an elite athlete is competing and follow their progress.

There are three levels of Elite badges:

results.title: JiuJitsu.net — Rankings, Videos, News and More
results.content:
This site is not affiliated, endorsed by, or associated with the International Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu Federation. More Info

 Live Brackets
 Registrations
 Archive

This tool imports brackets from bjjcompsystem.com and displays the current ratings of the competitors. Brackets are typically posted 1-2 days before an event starts.

New: Try this Chrome extension which adds our ratings and profile links to the IBJJF's "Order of Fights" page (see disclaimer).

 We cache responses from the IBJJF servers. If you don't see the latest data, try again in a few minutes.
 Ratings shown are estimates and may differ from an athlete's final rating at the end of an event.

results.title: JiuJitsu.net — Rankings, Videos, News and More
results.content:
This site is not affiliated, endorsed by, or associated with the International Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu Federation. More Info

## Yearly Team Awards - 2026 Gi

Click to see our top-ranked teams for the 2026 gi season

## Elite Athlete Lists

Search for a team to view elite athletes who have represented that team.

## Team Event Awards

Search for a past event in our database to view our team rankings based on match outcomes and opponent rating. To encourage competitive participation without pressure, white belts and teens are not included in team rankings.

results.title: Who to Watch at the Los Angeles Winter Open
results.content: The #1 seed is Pablo Lavaselli, and the Argentinian warrants closer attention. By our estimation, Lavaselli is not getting enough credit in the broader BJJ
